findings.evidence: `AlmostComplex.lean:303` defines `Invariant`; `CompatibleMetric.lean:64` uses only `h.invariant_apply`, line 74 uses the previous invariant-only lemma plus `associatedBilinForm_apply`, and line 79 combines that with `associatedBilinForm_apply_right_apply`.
findings.fix: State/prove these first from `hinv : ω.Invariant J` (with an `Invariant.invariant_apply` helper if useful), and derive any `Compatible` convenience lemmas from `h.invariant`.
findings.issue: The algebraic identities `associatedBilinForm_apply_left_apply`, `symplecticForm_eq_associatedBilinForm`, `associatedBilinForm_invariant`, and `associatedBilinForm_skewAdjoint` require `h : ω.Compatible J`, but their proofs only use `h.invariant`; positivity is unused.

findings.evidence: `AlmostComplex.lean:324` gives `ω.Tames J ↔ (ω.associatedBilinForm J).toQuadraticMap.PosDef`; `CompatibleMetric.lean:84` uses only `h.positive.nonneg`, and line 93 uses only `h.positive.anisotropic`.
findings.fix: Generalize these to `(ω.associatedBilinForm J).toQuadraticMap.PosDef` or the existing equivalent `ω.Tames J`, then derive the `Compatible` versions from `h.positive`/`h.tames`.
findings.issue: `associatedBilinForm_self_nonneg`, `symplecticForm_apply_self_nonneg`, and `associatedBilinForm_self_eq_zero` assume full `ω.Compatible J`, but only the positive-definite quadratic form field is used; invariance is unused.

summary: Several new theorem statements use full compatibility where the existing API has strictly weaker, natural hypotheses. Split the algebraic identities to `Invariant` and the positivity consequences to the existing positive-definite/taming hypotheses, then keep compatible wrappers only if needed.
